name: market_close_boundary
description: time_ratio = 1.0 (market close). Under the M3 re-derivation f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t), decay_curve = 1 - sqrt(0) = 1 EXACTLY, so dynamic_multiplier collapses to MULT_CLOSE and dynamic_min_stop collapses to MIN_STOP_CLOSE. Pins the upper-boundary regime: at close, the time-squeeze layer applies the tightest stop (0.5x vol multiplier, 0.15% minimum floor). Mirror of fixture 01: like t=0.0, a wrong impl that left the old log10 formula in place would also pass at t=1.0 (log10(10) is also 1) -- so this fixture is paired with the interior fixtures (03, 04, 05) where the two formulas diverge.
provenance: DERIVED from the closed-form THEORY formula f(t) = 1 - sqrt(1 - t) (Danielsson & Zigrand, 2003, LSE FMG DP-439). NOT captured from compute_time_squeeze_decay output -- D-2 load-bearing per docs/research/m3-provenance/literature-pass.md §3.1.
